zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Clifford於大約1254年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 奧 澤福時，她的父親Sir John de Clifford36歲，母親Margery Hereward32歲。她於大約1282年與Robert Fitz Payne Ist Baron Fitz Payne在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1319年之後在英國多瑟特馬爾申武德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Clifford于大约1254年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 奧 澤福时，她的父亲Sir John de Clifford36岁，母亲Margery Hereward32岁。她于大约1282年与Robert Fitz Payne Ist Baron Fitz Payne在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1319年之后在英国多瑟特馬爾申武德。

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Clifford was born about 1254, in Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Sir John de Clifford, was 36 and her mother, Margery Hereward, was 32. She married Robert Fitz Payne Ist Baron Fitz Payne about 1282, in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died after 1319, in Marshwood, Dorset, England.

Lei sposato Robert Fitz Payne Ist Baron Fitz Payne circa 1282, nel Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì dopo il 1319, nel Marshwood, Dorset, England.

ko.Frampton, Gloucestershire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프램프턴 비 시렌스스터
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Clifford(이)가 대략 1254년 영국 그루스스터실르 프램프턴  세버에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Clifford(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Hereward(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 대략 1282년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르에서 Robert Fitz Payne Ist Baron Fitz Payne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1319년 이후 영국 도르세트 마스h우드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Clifford nasceu aproximadamente 1254, em Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Clifford, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Margery Hereward, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Fitz Payne Ist Baron Fitz Payne aproximadamente 1282, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Clifford родилась около 1254 г. в местности фрамптон он сивирн, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Clifford было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Hereward, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Fitz Payne Ist Baron Fitz Payne около 1282 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 1319 г. в местности маршвуд, дорсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Clifford nació aproximadamente 1254, en Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Clifford, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Margery Hereward, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Robert Fitz Payne Ist Baron Fitz Payne aproximadamente 1282, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió después de 1319, en Marshwood, Dorset, Inglaterra.
